title: Censuring Representative Jasmine Crockett of Texas.
plain_summary: This bill is about officially criticizing Representative Jasmine Crockett from Texas for saying inappropriate things. She made bad comments about Texas Governor Greg Abbott and also said mean things about another member of Congress before. The House of Representatives wants to censure her, which means they will publicly scold her for her behavior. If this passes, she will have to stand in front of everyone in the House while the Speaker reads out loud why she is being punished.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ethics.

sections.title: Resolution Censuring Representative Jasmine Crockett of Texas
sections.text: This resolution says that Representative Jasmine Crockett of Texas should be censured (officially criticized and punished) by Congress. On March twenty-second, two thousand twenty-five, Representative Crockett spoke at a Human Rights Campaign event where she called Texas Governor Greg Abbott "Governor Hot Wheels" and "a hot ass mess." The resolution says these comments were inappropriate for a Member of Congress and discriminatory. It also mentions that Representative Crockett made inappropriate comments before, including on May sixteenth, two thousand twenty-four, during a Committee on Oversight and Accountability hearing where she made inflammatory comments about how another Member of Congress looked. The resolution orders three things: first, that Representative Crockett be censured; second, that she must come to the well (the open area in front of the Speaker's desk) of the House of Representatives to hear the censure announced; and third, that the Speaker must read this resolution out loud as part of the censure.
